raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California lost more than $810 million taxpayer dollars last year to unemployment insurance (UI) fraud.",
    "Legislation was signed into law to implement standard fraud-prevention practices at California's Employment Development Department (EDD).",
    "AB 110 requires EDD to cross-check unemployment claims against incarceration records to prevent paying fraudulent benefits.",
    "California law previously restricted sharing inmate information with other state agencies.",
    "AB 110 enables EDD to cross-check claimants' information against the database of inmate information maintained by the California Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation and by the counties.",
    "The current fraud situation involving state prisoner applicants is described as a breakdown of catastrophic proportions.",
    "AB 110 is essential in minimizing the loss of taxpayer dollars to fraud.",
    "Had a mechanism been in place to block fraud, millions of dollars would have been safeguarded.",
    "The California Chamber of Commerce supports AB 110 as it facilitates sharing of information between EDD and California's correctional system.",
    "Other states already employ the practice of cross-checking unemployment claims against incarceration data."
  ]
}
```
